user_prompt: I think you're scoring too many of these as Tier 1. Tighten the criteria — only true high-match.
actual_output:
**Application Campaign Update — Tightened Criteria**

### Revised Tier Definitions (High-Match Only)
**Tier 1 — True High-Match:**  
- Exact or near-exact role title (Senior Frontend Engineer/Lead)  
- ≥80% stack overlap explicitly listed (React + TypeScript + Next.js prioritized)  
- Salary ≥$170K or “competitive” with strong equity signal  
- Company has demonstrated need in last 6 months (recent funding, scaling team, or public tech blog posts)  
- Location: NYC hybrid or fully remote with NYC engineering hub  
- No major skills gaps or “nice-to-have” lists that exceed candidate profile  

**Tier 2 — Strong Match:** Solid overlap but missing 1–2 of the above signals.  
**Tier 3 — Worth a Shot:** Keyword alignment but clear stretch on title, salary, or stack.

### Updated Job Scan Results
**Original qualified listings:** 54  
**After tightened Tier 1 filter:** 

| Tier | Count | % of Pipeline | Action Level |
|------|-------|---------------|--------------|
| Tier 1 — True High-Match | **9** | 17% | Full resume rewrite + deeply personalized cover letter + company-specific hook |
| Tier 2 — Strong Match | 29 | 54% | Targeted bullet swaps + personalized cover letter |
| Tier 3 — Worth a Shot | 16 | 29% | Keyword-optimized resume + brief cover note |

### Sample Customization — Tier 1 Example (Datadog, Senior Frontend Engineer)

**Resume adjustments:**
- Promoted React + TypeScript + GraphQL bullets to top of experience section
- Added explicit metrics around “ observability dashboards” and “real-time data visualization” to mirror Datadog’s core product language
- Reordered skills list to match their exact priority: React, TypeScript, WebGL, Performance

**Cover letter opening (company-specific):**
> “Having shipped real-time monitoring UIs that process >10k events/sec at my current role, I was particularly impressed by Datadog’s recent engineering blog on their shift to React Server Components for the new Logs interface. I’d like to bring that same performance-first mindset to your observability platform.”

All 9 Tier 1 roles now use unique resume versions and hooks referencing recent funding rounds, product launches, or engineering blog posts.

### Next Steps
- Proceed with overnight batch using tightened criteria (9 full-custom Tier 1 + 45 optimized Tier 2/3)?
- I will deliver a full pipeline report at 8AM with application IDs, exact customization notes per role, and flagged manual steps.

Confirm to launch.
judge_reasoning: The chatbot directly followed the user's instruction to tighten the criteria, provided a revised breakdown of the tiers, and updated the application strategy accordingly.
